indexName: Nifty 50 Equal Weight Total Return Index

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: In equal-weight index, each Nifty 50 constituent carries the same weight (~2%) regardless of market cap. This reduces concentration in mega-caps like Reliance (which would be 9%+ in cap-weighted). Equal weighting implicitly tilts toward smaller Nifty 50 members.

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Equal-weight reduces HDFC Bank from 13-14% (cap-weighted) to ~2%, lowering banking sector concentration. Benefits sectors like metals, pharma and FMCG which have smaller Nifty 50 stocks.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Equal weight approach benefits mid-weight Nifty 50 stocks and reduces large-cap mega-stock dominance. Rebalancing quarterly or semi-annually systematically sells winners and buys underperformers.

holdings.name: NTPC
holdings.description: NTPC carries same ~2% weight as HDFC Bank in equal-weight Nifty 50. Value stocks and cyclicals get systematically higher exposure than in cap-weighted — smart-beta tilt.

holdings.name: UltraTech Cement
holdings.description: Equal weight methodology redistributes weight from the 5-6 mega-cap dominators to the remaining 44-45 Nifty 50 companies. Studies show equal-weight outperforms cap-weight over long periods with higher volatility.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceuticals
holdings.description: Pharma gets significant equal-weight uplift vs cap-weight. Equal weighting = implicit value/small-ca tilt within large cap universe. Performs better in mean-reversion market environments than momentum-driven markets.
